data.title: Resolution and reporter enum; Test-fixture hygiene and purge
data.description:
## Context

Three attribution gaps converge here. First, bug closure today requires a freeform `--reason` string with narrow prefix rules (`Fixed:` or `Escalated to user:`) — unstructured, unqueryable, lossy to Jira. Jira ships five default resolutions (Fixed / Won\t Fix / Duplicate / Incomplete / Cannot Reproduce) that map directly onto agent use-cases. Second, the local `author` field conflates two distinct concepts: the human who opened the ticket vs the agent/skill/event that precipitated it. Replacing `author` with a typed `reporter` enum (User / Jira / Agent:Skill:<name> / Agent:Skill:Unknown / Agent:Deferral / Agent:Gotcha / Agent:Suggestion / Agent:Coordination) gives both Jira-compatible attribution (accountId for humans) and DSO-local agent provenance (trigger source for agents). Third, the real tickets tracker currently holds 336 open (plus 1,571 archived) tickets authored by `Test` — leakage from test fixtures into the production tracker. These must be purged and prevented.

## Success Criteria
- New `resolution` field written as part of STATUS=closed event; enum: `Fixed` | `Wont_Fix` | `Duplicate` | `Incomplete` | `Cannot_Reproduce`. Jira-compatible names.
- Bug close no longer requires freeform `--reason`; `--resolution` replaces it as the structured field. `--reason` becomes optional free-text sugar (blank allowed, historical tickets preserved unchanged). Migration maps existing "Fixed: X" -> resolution=Fixed,reason=X; "Escalated to user: X" -> resolution=Incomplete,reason=X.
- `author` field renamed to `reporter` with enum above. Backfill migration: `Claude` -> `Agent:Skill:Unknown`, `Joe Oakhart`/`Main` -> `User`, `Test` -> DELETE ticket (not mapped), `github-actions[bot]` -> `Agent:Coordination`, `<missing>` -> `User`.
- Guard: `ticket create` rejects `--reporter=Test*` unless `DSO_TEST_FIXTURE=1` environment variable is set. Pre-commit hook scans for Test-authored tickets in `.tickets-tracker/` and fails if any are present.
- Force-delete of all 336 open + 1,571 archived `Test`-authored tickets from the real tracker in a single atomic migration commit.
- Bridge outbound writes resolution via Jira REST API `transitions.resolution`; inbound reads Jira resolution field and maps to local enum. `reporter=Jira` and `reporter=Agent:*` values sync to Jira as labels (since Jira assignee/reporter require accountId).

## Approach
Four migrations land in sequence within this epic: (1) reducer schema extension to add `resolution` and `reporter` fields with defaults; (2) CLI surface extension — new `--resolution` flag on transition, new `--reporter` flag on create, deprecation of `--reason`; (3) backfill migration that reads all existing events and emits EDIT events mapping the legacy fields; (4) Test-ticket purge + guard hook. Bridge outbound/inbound handlers in `_outbound_handlers.py` and `_inbound_api.py` are extended to include resolution in EDIT tracking.

## Dependencies
- No upstream epic dependencies.
- Epic 7 (Jira bridge sync remediation) depends on this epic (reducer/schema prerequisite).

## Out of Scope
- Changing Jira assignee/reporter accountId mapping (handled by Epic 7)
- Agent-specific Jira user provisioning (not planned)
